branches.label: Quantitative Reasoning
branches.detail: Tests basic numerical ability — number systems, arithmetic, and data interpretation at a level accessible without a dedicated math background, since CUET draws candidates from all streams.

children.label: Number System
children.detail: Properties of integers, divisibility, and basic number operations.

children.label: HCF & LCM
children.detail: Finding highest common factor and lowest common multiple, including word problems on grouping and timing.

children.label: Simplification
children.detail: Applying BODMAS correctly to solve multi-step numerical expressions quickly.

children.label: Arithmetic
children.detail: Everyday calculation problems built on ratios and averages.

children.label: Ratio & Proportion
children.detail: Comparing quantities and solving proportion-based word problems.

children.label: Average
children.detail: Calculating mean values across data sets, including weighted averages.

children.label: Data Interpretation
children.detail: Reading and computing from tabulated or graphical data under time pressure.

children.label: Tables
children.detail: Extracting and comparing values from structured tabular data.

children.label: Graphs
children.detail: Reading trends and values from line, bar, or pie graphs.

branches.label: Logical Reasoning
branches.detail: Tests analytical and visual-pattern thinking rather than subject knowledge, making it one of the more practice-friendly sections of CUET's General Test.

children.label: Analytical
children.detail: Statement-based deductive reasoning.

children.label: Statement & Conclusion
children.detail: Judging whether a given conclusion logically follows from a stated premise.

children.label: Assumptions
children.detail: Identifying the implicit assumption underlying a given statement or argument.

children.label: Visual
children.detail: Figure and pattern-based reasoning.

children.label: Pattern Series
children.detail: Identifying the next figure or number in a visual or numeric sequence.

children.label: Odd One Out
children.detail: Spotting the figure or item that doesn't share the common pattern with the rest of the group.

branches.label: General Knowledge
branches.detail: Covers static facts and current events. Breadth matters more than depth here, since questions are typically one-liners rather than analytical.

children.label: Static GK
children.detail: Fixed factual knowledge that doesn't change year to year.

children.label: Books & Authors
children.detail: Notable books and their authors, frequently asked in a direct match format.

children.label: Important Days
children.detail: National and international observance days and their significance.

children.label: Current Affairs
children.detail: Recent events from roughly the past year, the most volatile and high-yield-if-prepared part of GK.

children.label: National
children.detail: Major domestic policy, political, and economic developments.

children.label: International
children.detail: Significant global events, summits, and agreements.

children.label: Sports
children.detail: Recent tournament results, award winners, and record-breaking performances.

children.label: Static India
children.detail: Fixed geographical and administrative facts about India.

children.label: States & Capitals
children.detail: Indian states/union territories with their capitals — a guaranteed easy mark if memorized.

children.label: National Parks
children.detail: Major national parks and wildlife sanctuaries and the species they're known for.

branches.label: Reading Comprehension
branches.detail: Tests reading speed and inference from an unseen passage — a skill best built through regular reading practice rather than last-minute prep.

children.label: Passage Based
children.detail: Direct and indirect questions drawn from a given paragraph.

children.label: Inference
children.detail: Drawing conclusions that are implied but not directly stated in the passage.

children.label: Vocabulary in Context
children.detail: Determining a word's meaning based on how it's used within the passage.
